title: Start
empty_state_description: Connect a GitHub repository to get started.
repo_explanation: This repository has been imported into the compositor. If you would like to select a different one, click the 'Connect Repo' button above.
intro_paragraph_1: Telar is a digital storytelling platform that combines high-resolution images, audio, and video with step-by-step narratives. Your site is built around two primary content types: objects — images served via the International Image Interoperability Framework (IIIF), plus audio and video, self-hosted or sourced from museums and libraries worldwide — and stories — step-by-step narratives that guide visitors through those objects, combining close-up views with questions, answers, and expandable layers of deeper information. These are complemented by glossary entries (key terms your stories can link to for quick definitions) and pages (supporting content such as an About or Credits section).
intro_paragraph_2: Use the compositor to manage all of this content, configure your site, and publish changes. You can learn more about how Telar works at <telarLink>telar.org</telarLink> and in the <docsLink>documentation</docsLink>.

workflow.title: How the compositor works
workflow.step1_desc: Set up your site's title, description, language, and display options.
workflow.step2_desc: Manage the images your stories reference — add metadata, check IIIF tiles, and import external sources.
workflow.step3_desc: Create and edit step-by-step narratives with close-up views, annotations, and expandable information layers.
workflow.step4_desc: Add a glossary of terms, customise your homepage, and create supporting pages to enrich your stories.
workflow.step5_desc: Review your changes and publish to your GitHub Pages site in a single commit.

save_publish: Your edits are saved automatically as you work. Nothing goes live on your published site until you choose to publish. If someone edits the repository directly on GitHub, you'll need to re-sync before publishing.
preview_intro: Below is a preview of your site's landing page. Each section shows your actual content as visitors will see it — click any section to edit it directly. You can also use the tabs above to manage your stories, objects, and site configuration in more detail.

preview.site_description_explanation: Your site's title and description, as they appear on the landing page and in search results.
preview.welcome_explanation: The introductory content visitors see when they arrive at your site. This comes from your site's index page.
preview.stories_explanation: Your story collection as it appears on the landing page. Drag to reorder — this controls the display order on your published site.
preview.stories_intro_placeholder: Add an optional intro for the stories section
preview.stories_intro_hint: Optional — shown above your stories if you add it.
preview.objects_explanation: Featured objects from your collection, displayed on the landing page.
preview.empty_default_hint: Empty fields show your site's localised default.

status_bar.never_hint: Edit here, then publish to your site when ready
status_bar.manage_repos: Switch, add, or remove GitHub Repos

hint: Drag to reorder — this controls the order on your published site

delete_story.title: Delete story
delete_story.body: Delete '{{title}}'? This will remove the story and its {{count}} steps from the compositor. Your published site won't change until you publish again.

soft_limit.stories: You have {{count}} stories. Performance may be affected with more than 10.
soft_limit.projects: You have {{count}} projects. The limit is 5 connected repositories.

sync_modal.title: Repository has changed
sync_modal.confirm_body: Your GitHub repository has been updated since the last time it was synced with the compositor. Review what changed to make sure our working copy stays in sync with the published site.
sync_modal.conflict_warning: You have {{count}} unpublished change(s). Syncing will overwrite them with the repository version.
sync_modal.no_changes_body: GitHub has commits to files the compositor doesn't manage (README, /docs, LICENSE). Your sync baseline has been updated — no action needed.
sync_modal.use_compositor_helper: Updates the sync baseline to GitHub's HEAD without re-importing. Your compositor work and the GitHub edits will land together at the next publish.

orphan_banner.count_text: Found {{count}} unreferenced story file(s) on GitHub.
